title: `--mount=type=bind,relabel=shared` not working: `lsetxattr` ... operation not supported.
body:
### Issue Description

The `relabel=shared` option isn't supported with a bind mount, contrary to [the man page of `podman run`](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#mount-type-type-type-specific-option).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```console 
$ podman \
  run \
    --rm \
    --userns keep-id:uid=10001,gid=10001 \
    --mount=type=bind,bind-nonrecursive,consistency=delegated,relabel=shared,source="$(pwd)",target=/builds \
          docker.io/library/alpine \
    touch /builds/blabla
```

### Describe the results you received

```text
Error: preparing container 0711bab605a0374077dc6feb5b840ee801b39935236836d10b0b57779902c415 for attach: lsetxattr /mypwdpath/.cargo: operation not supported
```

### Describe the results you expected

Container created.

comments.body: This usually indicates you are trying to relabel a file system which is not allowed to be relabeled.
A user friendly fault message would be helpful. I don't know which filesystem, whether this applies to a filesystem within the Podman Desktop VM, or directly on macOS.
You don't know where `/mypwdpath/.cargo` came from?
Remember Podman inside of VM has no idea where the source path on the host came from, only the volume inside of the VM.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This should work now with podman machine AFAIK
